statements.name: PROC CAEFFECT
statements.help: PROC CAEFFECT <options>; BY variables; DIFFERENCE <REFLEV=(reference-list)><EVTLEV=(event-list) > ; DISPLAY <table-list></ options>; DISPLAYOUT table-spec-list </ options>; FREQ variable; OUTCOMEMODEL RESTORE=libref.data-table PREDNAME=prediction-name; OUTCOMEVAR outcome-variable <(EVENT='event-level')></outcome-options> ; POM TREATLEV='treatment-level'<PREDOUT=predicted-outcome-value><TREATPROB=predicted-treatment-probability> ; TREATVAR treatment-variable</treatment-options> ; WEIGHT variable;

arguments.name: DATA=
arguments.description: names the input data table for PROC CAEFFECT to use

arguments.name: INFERENCE
arguments.description: computes standard errors and confidence intervals for the potential outcome mean and causal effect estimates. Note that because the CAEFFECT procedure takes a model-agnostic approach to causal effect estimation, the statistical validity of the inference depends on model-specific conditions that you must evaluate and that the procedure cannot verify.

arguments.name: METHOD=
arguments.description: specifies the method to use for estimating potential outcome means (POMs)

arguments.name: AIPW
arguments.description: estimates POMs by augmented inverse probability weighting. This method requires the observed values of the outcome, predicted treatment probabilities, and predicted counterfactual outcome values.

arguments.name: IPW
arguments.description: estimates POMs by inverse probability weighting. This method requires the observed values of the outcome and predicted treatment probabilities.

arguments.name: REGADJ
arguments.description: estimates POMs by regression adjustment. This method requires predicted counterfactual outcome values.

arguments.name: TMLE
arguments.description: { estimates POMs by targeted maximum likelihood estimation

arguments.name: POMCOV
arguments.description: displays a covariance matrix of the potential outcome mean estimates. Note that because the CAEFFECT procedure takes a model-agnostic approach to causal effect estimation, the statistical validity of the inference depends on model-specific conditions that you must evaluate and that the procedure cannot verify.

arguments.name: POMINFO
arguments.description: displays the "Potential Outcome Mean Information" table

arguments.name: SCALEDIPWFLAG=
arguments.description: specifies a multiple of the expected inverse probability weight of an observation that is used to flag observations that have large weights

statements.name: BY
statements.description: produces separate analyses of observations in groups that are defined by the BY variables

statements.name: DIFFERENCE
statements.description: specifies causal effects to estimate on the difference scale

arguments.name: EVTLEV=
arguments.description: specifies event levels for causal effect estimates. The levels of the of treatment that you specify in event-list must also be used to define a potential outcome in a POM statement.

arguments.name: REFLEV=
arguments.description: specifies reference levels for causal effect estimates. The levels of the of treatment that you specify in reference-list must also be used to define a potential outcome in a POM statement.

statements.name: DISPLAY
statements.description: enables you to specify a list of display tables to display or exclude

arguments.name: CASESENSITIVE
arguments.description: performs a case-sensitive comparison of table names in the table-list to display table names when tables are subsetted for display

arguments.name: EXCLUDE
arguments.description: displays all display tables except those that you specify in the table-list

arguments.name: EXCLUDEALL
arguments.description: suppresses display of all tables

arguments.name: TRACE
arguments.description: displays the display table names, labels, and paths

statements.name: DISPLAYOUT
statements.description: enables you to create output tables from your displayed output
statements.help: DISPLAYOUT table-spec-list </ options>; table-spec-list specifies a list of output tables in key=value or key format key=value specifies key as the ODS table name, path, or partial pathname, and value as the output table name key is the ODS table name and the output table name

arguments.name: INCLUDEALL
arguments.description: creates output tables for all display tables

arguments.name: NOREPLACE
arguments.description: does not replace any existing output table of the same name

arguments.name: REPEATED
arguments.description: replicates all output tables on all nodes

statements.name: FREQ
statements.description: identifies a numeric variable in the input data table that contains the frequency of occurrence of each observation

statements.name: OUTCOMEMODEL
statements.description: enables you to use a previously fit model for the outcome variable to compute counterfactual outcomes

arguments.name: RESTORE=
arguments.description: specifies the saved model in the form of an analytic store

arguments.name: PREDNAME=
arguments.description: identifies the variable that contains the outcome of interest when data are scored using the analytic store

statements.name: OUTCOMEVAR
statements.description: specifies information about the outcome variable

arguments.name: COUNTMISSING
arguments.description: treats missing values (".", ".A", . . . , ".Z" for numeric variables and blanks for character variables) as valid values of a categorical outcome variable.

arguments.name: LEVELIZERAW
arguments.description: bases the levelization of a categorical outcome variable on raw values

arguments.name: MAXLEV=
arguments.description: specifies the maximum number of levels for a categorical outcome variable

arguments.name: TYPE=
arguments.description: specifies the type of the outcome variable

arguments.name: BINOMIAL
arguments.description: specifies a binomial outcome. For a binomial outcome, the event probability is modeled, and predicted outcome values must be between 0 and 1, inclusive. If the specified estimation method requires the observed outcome values, you must use event/trial syntax.

arguments.name: CATEGORICAL
arguments.description: specifies a categorical outcome. For a categorical outcome, the probability of a designated event level is modeled, and the observed outcome and predicted outcome values must be between 0 and 1, inclusive. If the specified estimation method requires the observed outcome values, you must specify the event level by using the EVENT= option.

arguments.name: CONTINUOUS
arguments.description: specifies a continuous outcome

arguments.name: EVENT=
arguments.description: specifies the event-level of interest for a categorical outcome

statements.name: POM
statements.description: specifies a potential outcome mean to estimate

arguments.name: TREATLEV=
arguments.description: specifies the level of the treatment variable that defines the potential outcome. If the treatment variable is a character variable, specify the treatment-level in quotes.

arguments.name: PREDOUT=
arguments.description: specifies the input variable that contains the predicted counterfactual outcome values for the specified treatment level

arguments.name: TREATPROB=
arguments.description: specifies the input variable that contains the predicted probability of having the specified level of treatment

statements.name: TREATVAR
statements.description: specifies information about the treatment variable

arguments.name: CONDEVENT=
arguments.description: specifies the estimation of conditional potential outcome means and the observed treatment-level to condition on. This treatment-level must appear in a potential outcome specification. Conditional potential outcome mean estimation is supported only for the IPW and REGADJ estimation methods.

arguments.name: COUNTMISSING
arguments.description: treats missing values (".", ".A", . . . , ".Z" for numeric variables and blanks for character variables) as valid values of the treatment variable.

arguments.name: LEVELIZERAW
arguments.description: bases the levelization of the treatment variable on raw values

arguments.name: MAXLEV=
arguments.description: specifies the maximum number of levels for the treatment variable

statements.name: WEIGHT
statements.description: The variable that you specify in the WEIGHT statement is used as a weight to perform a weighted analysis of the dat
